file.content:
name: Deployed Smoke

# suite 22/P03 — runs the deployed-environment smoke suite whenever a
# Vercel (or any GitHub-integrated) production deployment reports
# success. The job is also runnable manually so the site can be probed
# on demand.

on:
  deployment_status:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      url:
        description: "Target URL (overrides deployment_status payload)"
        required: false
        type: string

jobs:
  smoke:
    # Only fire for successful Production deployments. workflow_dispatch
    # always runs.
    if: >-
      github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' ||
      (github.event.deployment_status.state == 'success' &&
       github.event.deployment.environment == 'Production')
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 10
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version-file: ".nvmrc"
          cache: npm

      - name: Install
        run: npm ci

      - name: Install Playwright browsers
        run: npx playwright install --with-deps chromium

      - name: Resolve target URL
        id: target
        run: |
          if [ -n "${{ inputs.url }}" ]; then
            echo "url=${{ inputs.url }}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          else
            echo "url=${{ github.event.deployment_status.target_url }}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          fi

      - name: Run smoke
        env:
          SMOKE_URL: ${{ steps.target.outputs.url }}
        run: npm run smoke

      - name: Upload smoke results
        if: always()
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: smoke-results
          path: |
            smoke-results.json
            playwright-report/
            test-results/
          retention-days: 7

name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:

concurrency:
  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: ${{ github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' }}

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Setup Node
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version-file: ".nvmrc"
          cache: npm

      - name: Install
        run: npm ci

      - name: Lint
        run: npm run lint

      - name: Typecheck
        run: npm run typecheck

      - name: Build
        run: npm run build

  e2e:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: build
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with: { node-version-file: ".nvmrc", cache: npm }
      - run: npm ci
      - name: Install Playwright browsers
        run: npx playwright install --with-deps chromium
      - name: Build
        run: npm run build
      - name: Smoke test
        run: npm run test:e2e

1	/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
2	 * ThemeInit — pre-hydration theme injection (suite 14/P01)
3	 *
4	 * Renders an inline <script> that reads localStorage "theme" (or falls back
5	 * to prefers-color-scheme) and sets data-theme on <html> before paint, so
6	 * we never flash the wrong theme during hydration.
7	 * --------------------------------------------------------------------------- */
8	
9	// Mirrors P07 §B3 exactly. Inlined into <head> via dangerouslySetInnerHTML so
10	// data-theme lands on <html> before paint, eliminating FOUC.
11	const SCRIPT = `(function(){try{var saved=localStorage.getItem("theme");var sys=window.matchMedia("(prefers-color-scheme: light)").matches?"light":"dark";var effective=(saved==="light"||saved==="dark")?saved:sys;document.documentElement.setAttribute("data-theme",effective);}catch(e){}})();`;
12	
13	export function ThemeInit() {
14	  return <script dangerouslySetInnerHTML={{ __html: SCRIPT }} />;
15	}
16	
17	export default ThemeInit;

name: Visual Regression

# suite 22/P04 — diffs the visual baselines committed under
# e2e/__screenshots__/ against a fresh capture of the production build.
# Failures upload the diff/expected/actual triplet so a maintainer can
# eyeball the drift before re-baselining via `npm run visual:update`.

on:
  pull_request:
  workflow_dispatch:

concurrency:
  group: visual-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

jobs:
  visual:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 20
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version-file: ".nvmrc"
          cache: npm

      - name: Install
        run: npm ci

      - name: Install Playwright browsers
        run: npx playwright install --with-deps chromium

      - name: Build
        run: npm run build

      - name: Visual diff
        run: npm run visual

      - name: Upload visual diffs
        if: always()
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: visual-diffs
          path: |
            test-results/
            playwright-report/
          retention-days: 14

content.text:
## Results

**Files created/touched**
- `e2e/visual.spec.ts` — 32 generated tests (8 routes × 2 themes × 2 viewports), per-test setup: viewport → reduced-motion + colorScheme emulation → seeded `localStorage.theme` via `addInitScript` → `goto(networkidle)` → assert `html[data-theme]` → inject blanket `transition/animation: 0s` style → await `document.fonts.ready` → mask `[data-volatile]` → `toHaveScreenshot(name, { fullPage, animations:"disabled", caret:"hide", maxDiffPixelRatio: 0.001, mask })`.
- `playwright.config.ts` — added `snapshotPathTemplate: "{testDir}/__screenshots__/{arg}{ext}"` so baselines collapse into a single flat dir.
- `package.json` — added `"visual"` and `"visual:update"` scripts.
- `.github/workflows/visual.yml` — runs on PR + manual dispatch; build → `npm run visual` → uploads `test-results/` and `playwright-report/` artifacts on failure.
- `e2e/__screenshots__/` — 32 PNG baselines captured locally and committed.

**Choice notes**
- Used Playwright's built-in `toHaveScreenshot` (the screenshot-specialized variant of `toMatchSnapshot`) rather than third-party Percy/Chromatic/Argos — zero external vendor dependency, satisfies §D.
- `MAX_DIFF_RATIO = 0.001` = 0.1 % pixel tolerance per B4.
- `[data-volatile]` selector reserved as a forward-compat mask hook; the chat surface currently emits no timestamps, so no real elements are masked today.

**Verification**
- `test -f e2e/visual.spec.ts` → exists.
- `ls e2e/__screenshots__/ | wc -l` → 32.
- Capture pass: 32/32 wrote actuals.
- Re-run pass (no `--update-snapshots`): 32/32 matched baselines (6.1 s).

**B6 caveat (per spec)**: baselines were captured on darwin-arm64. CI runs Linux Chromium — minor font-hinting / sub-pixel anti-aliasing drift may appear on the first CI run. The 0.1 % tolerance absorbs jitter; if blanket drift exceeds tolerance, re-baseline by running `npm run visual:update` in a Linux environment and committing the results.

IMPL_RESULT: PASS
